name: Sausage-Stuffed Zucchini
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Tomatoes, peeled, seeded and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the ends off each zucchini. Cut the center portions of each zucchini into 2-inch lengths. Cut the remaining ends into half-inch rounds.
2. Hollow out the centers of each zucchini length using a knife or melon baller. The hollowed-out portions should be just large enough to hold one small link of sausage; reserve the hollowed-out pulp.
3. Roll each sausage in cayenne pepper.
4. Heat oil in a skillet and add the sausages. Cook, turning often, until browned and cooked through. Sprinkle with anise seeds, coating the sausages evenly.
5. Stuff one sausage into each piece of zucchini. Place the stuffed pieces back into the skillet. Add the small zucchini rounds and reserved hollowed-out pulp.
6. Combine water, turmeric, cumin, paprika, coriander, garlic, tomatoes, and salt to taste. Pour over the zucchini. Cook, turning the zucchini pieces often, about 5 minutes. Cover closely and continue cooking for another 30 minutes. If the water becomes too low as the zucchini cook, add up to half a cup more.
7. Once ready, the zucchini should be very tender, and the sauce reduced to about one-third cup. Remove and discard garlic cloves. Serve hot or cold.
